title: Add Persistent Floating Music Player and Settings Page with Background Music

requirements.text: Create a persistent floating music player component (frontend/src/components/FloatingMusicPlayer.tsx) fixed to the bottom-right corner of all pages. The player must: (1) embed and play YouTube tracks via the YouTube IFrame API in a hidden iframe; (2) include a pre-defined Sonic track rotation in order: Angel Island Zone Act 1, Angel Island Zone Act 2, Chemical Plant Zone, Green Hill Zone, Emerald Hill Zone, Ice Cap Zone, Flying Battery Zone, Hydrocity Zone — each mapped to a real YouTube video ID; (3) render left (⬅️) and right (➡️) arrow buttons to navigate to the previous/next track, wrapping around at the ends; (4) render an On/Off toggle button to enable or disable music playback; (5) render a volume slider from 0 to 100 with left/right nudge buttons (each nudge adjusts volume by 5 units); (6) auto-loop the current track when it ends; (7) persist current track index, volume level, and on/off state in sessionStorage so the player state survives navigation between pages within the session; (8) display the current track name above the controls.

quotes: Add background music and add options to turn on or off background music have options for background music as angel island act 1 on loop and ad they press the arrow right it switched the music to angel island act 2
when they press volume button left is to turn it down and 0 means muted and 100 means loud and they have options to slide the slider to a decent percentage of volume on music
The music will be background music

acceptanceCriteria: Floating player is visible and accessible on every page of the app
Player is fixed to the bottom-right corner and does not obstruct main content
Right arrow advances to the next track; left arrow goes to the previous track
Track wraps from last to first and first to last when cycling
On/Off toggle starts and stops playback
Volume slider ranges from 0 (muted) to 100 (full volume)
Left and right nudge buttons adjust volume by 5 units per click
Track auto-loops when it reaches the end
Current track name is displayed in the player UI
Player state (track index, volume, on/off) is restored from sessionStorage after navigating between pages

requirements.text: Mount the FloatingMusicPlayer component in the root App layout (frontend/src/App.tsx or frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx) so it renders persistently on all pages regardless of route.

quotes: The music will be background music

acceptanceCriteria: FloatingMusicPlayer is rendered on every route including HomePage, AdminPanel, ModeratorDashboard, UserStatus, and any Settings page
Player does not unmount or reset when the user navigates between pages

requirements.text: Create a new Settings page (frontend/src/pages/SettingsPage.tsx) accessible from the main navigation to all authenticated users. The page must include a 'Background Music' section that: (1) lists all tracks in the Sonic rotation (same 8 tracks as the floating player) with a 'Select' button per track to set it as the active track; (2) highlights the currently active track; (3) provides an 'Import YouTube URL' input field and an 'Add Track' button — when a valid YouTube URL is submitted, it is parsed for the video ID and appended to the user's track rotation for the session; (4) includes a volume slider (0–100) and on/off toggle mirrored from the floating player state (changes here update the floating player and vice versa via shared sessionStorage state); (5) displays an error if an invalid or empty YouTube URL is submitted.

quotes: in settings account and background music and access music settings and you will see the tracks or you can import a song or video
Import source: Users paste a YouTube URL to add custom tracks
Settings page: The Background Music section lives inside a new dedicated Settings page accessible from the main navigation

acceptanceCriteria: Settings page is linked from the main navigation and accessible to all authenticated users
Background Music section lists all 8 Sonic tracks with individual Select buttons
Currently active track is visually highlighted
Selecting a track from the list switches the floating player to that track
YouTube URL input field accepts a pasted URL; clicking 'Add Track' appends the track to the rotation
Invalid or empty URL submissions show an inline error message
Volume slider and on/off toggle on the Settings page reflect and update the floating player state
Changes made on the Settings page are immediately reflected in the floating player

requirements.text: Add a 'Settings' navigation link in the main app header/navbar (frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx) that routes to the new Settings page. This link must be visible to all authenticated users.

quotes: Settings page accessible from the main navigation

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Settings' link appears in the main navigation for all authenticated users
Clicking the link navigates to the Settings page
The Settings link is not shown to unauthenticated users

requirements.text: Register the Settings page route in the TanStack Router configuration (frontend/src/App.tsx) at a path such as '/settings', wrapped in an AuthGuard so only authenticated users can access it.

quotes: new dedicated Settings page accessible from the main navigation

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /settings renders the SettingsPage component
Unauthenticated users are redirected away from /settings by the AuthGuard
The route is included in the TanStack Router tree

constraints: Do not modify any files listed under frontend.immutablePaths
All YouTube playback must use the YouTube IFrame API (no direct audio file uploads or streaming)
No backend changes are required for this feature — all music state is client-side (sessionStorage)
The floating music player must not obstruct or overlap critical UI elements such as modals or action buttons
nonGoals: Storing music preferences in the backend or on-chain
Uploading local audio or video files from the user's device
Streaming licensed audio files directly (only YouTube embeds)
Showing the music player to unauthenticated users
Any changes to existing moderation, admin, or user-status features
